signatures.title: Signature Reference
signatures.shipExplainer: In ship mining the scan signature identifies the dominant mineral directly — one value, one mineral. Rocks additionally contain secondary minerals. The scanner shows the sum of a cluster: base signature × number of rocks (e.g. 18,000 = 5 × Bexalite 3,600). The lower the base signature, the rarer the mineral.
signatures.groundExplainer: For ground deposits the signature only indicates the size of the deposit — it does not identify the mineral. Each cluster is 100% one single mineral; which one depends on the location. The scanner sums here too (e.g. 8,000 = 2 ROC deposits).
signatures.collisionNote: Note: signature 3000 is shared with capital wreck debris (890 Jump class) — context decides: planet surface = deposit, open space = debris.
signatures.oreGroundNote: The signature does not identify the mineral — check the location's mineral list instead.
signatures.oreClusterHint: Cluster: {x2} (×2) · {x3} (×3) · {x4} (×4) — the scanner shows base signature × rock count.
signatures.intro: What to look for when scanning in Star Citizen: rock signatures for ship mining and deposit sizes for ROC and FPS mining — with community-verified in-game RS values.
